items.title: 'I didn't feel safe': Mum reveals concerns before childcare closure

children.content: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

image.altText: A mother stands with a serious expression with her two children.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

image.altText: Football teammates celebrating.

items.title: These lemurs like to 'move it, move it' to the sound of a harp

children.content: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

gallery.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

thumbnail.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

items.title: Hume Highway reopens after truck driver dies in crash

children.content: A man has died after a fiery crash on the Hume Highway this morning, where two trucks collided and left part of the highway closed for hours.

image.altText: A plume of smoke behind a fire truck on a road in the dark

items.title: ACT minister responds to claim that hospital turned NSW patient away

children.content: ACT Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says a misunderstanding led a NSW patient to believe they had been denied healthcare in Canberra because they lived over the border.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Canberra childcare centre closes suddenly, worker sacked

children.content: Parents at Edge Early Learning in Higgins received an email last night advising the regulator had ordered the centre to immediately shut.

items.title: Driver charged over fiery multi-truck crash near Goulburn

children.content: A 28-year-old semi-trailer driver is charged with negligent driving over an explosive collision that saw six trucks destroyed in a fire at a rest stop near Goulburn.

image.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

items.title: Hanson quiet on who paid for Italy trip before speaking at pricey dinner

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson refuses to say who paid for her recent trip to Italy, where she attended a fashion show and was spotted at a five-star resort.

articles.title: Malcolm Roberts falsely claimed COVID-19 vaccines caused miscarriages

children.content: The One Nation senator made the previously unreported remarks during a live stream discussion with three prominent Australian anti-vaccine mandate activists.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson and Malcolm Roberts

items.title: 'Bring back public flogging': Inquiry hears of vicious online trolling

children.content: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

image.altText: Racism Hearing Inquiry committee

items.title: Going after the media a tried and true tactic of diversion

children.content: A minister of the Crown's scolding of a journalist asking a legitimate question about firearm ownership, and the subsequent pile-on allowed to run on social media, should ring alarm bells for anyone concerned with democracy.

image.altText: A bearded man speaking at press conference.

avatar.alt: A woman with glasses stands in a TV studio.

items.title: Lao authorities charge distillery owners over fatal methanol poisoning

children.content: Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles, both 19, died after the incident at the Nana Backpackers Hostel in Vang Vieng in November 2024.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: Sydney suburbs which recorded rent price drops, as 'real test' looms

children.content: The June quarterly rent report from Domain paints a mixed picture of Sydney's market, including both major surges and significant drops for rent prices in certain suburbs.

image.altText: For lease for rent signs in Sydney in front of apartments and houses.

items.title: Antisemitism inquiry turns uncomfortable lens on university protests

children.content: Universities have apologised to Jewish staff and students who have spoken of their distress at being targeted by protest movement.

gallery.altText: A group of protesters cheering and waving flags.

items.title: Melbourne residents resist public housing relocation plan

children.content: An Albert Park tower block is shaping up as the next battleground for the Victorian government, as part of its controversial rebuild of public housing towers.

image.altText: An elderly lady with long silver hair sits on an armchair in a cluttered apartment

items.title: Former prosecutor says charges likely for man who shot alleged intruder

children.content: A suspected home invasion in northern NSW that left one man shot and an elderly couple with stab wounds sparks renewed discussion about self-defence and so-called "castle law".

image.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

items.title: Sam Neill's advice for when the world seems too dark

children.content: The world has been remembering Sam Neill's incredible film and television career in the wake of the actor's death, but his off-screen antics left a robust impact.

items.title: Failed interns get a second chance to become doctors in new Aussie drama

children.content: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

image.altText: Ioane Sa'ula, Samoan Australian, mid-20s, in orange scrubs, sits in a hospital waiting area, leaning forward, hands clasped.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Sam Neill's Marvel cameo, 70s punk rock and traffic cones

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: What comes next after the landmark BHP strike?

children.content: For the first time in more than two decades, BHP workers in the the Pilbara went on strike. What do we know about how much impact it had and what comes next?

items.title: 'Painful and hard process': Church votes to bar women from senior roles

children.content: A vote excluding women from elder positions in the Presbyterian Church in NSW and the ACT is disappointing but not surprising, a long-standing parishioner says.

image.altText: Presbyterian Church parishioner Sonia Thew

items.title: The LNP's new crime statistics could be comparing 'apples and oranges'

items.title: Australian stuns The Open with equal lowest round in major golf history

children.content: Australia's Lucas Herbert takes a shock lead at The Open after posting a stunning round where he birdied six of his opening nine holes.

items.title: The quote that shows the divide within Australia's Comm Games team

children.content: While a Commonwealth Games gold rush is always expected for Australia's swimmers, the emerging generation of track and field athletes see Glasgow as a chance to make their mark on a global stage.

image.altText: Oliver Hoare shouts to celebrate winning the 1,500m at the 2022 Birmingham Commonwealth Games.

children.content: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

items.title: More than 20 SA Department for Child Protection employees stood down

items.title: Artist behind Clinton portrait in Epstein files remembers first piece

children.content: A high school art portrait launched Petrina Ryan-Kleid's career. Forty years and several satirical portraits later, she has been reunited with person who bought her first work.

items.title: Burnham to become next UK PM after Australian candidate gets one vote

children.content: Andy Burnham will be Britain's seventh prime minister in 10 years next week after winning a Labour leadership contest in which his only challenger, an Australian, received just one vote.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiling and looking on. A microphone is also visible.

items.title: Smelter clean-up bill likely to be funded by Tasmanian taxpayers, experts say

children.content: The clean-up bill for the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter site may ultimately may be paid by the Tasmanian government, according to legal experts, following administrators' failure to secure a buyer for the embattled operation.

image.altText: Worker inside a smelter factory.

items.title: Brenda Fricker, Oscar-winning Irish actress, dies aged 81

children.content: Brenda Fricker became the first Irish actress to win an Oscar for the 1989 film My Left Foot and also played the kindly Pigeon Lady in Home Alone 2.
